summary: Frontend-only test for Jambh Electricals WebView wrapper (no backend). Verified: (1) app loads without errors on Expo web preview at EXPO_PACKAGER_PROXY_URL; (2) new compact premium WebPreviewCard renders with logo, title 'Jambh Electricals', teal accent tag 'NATIVE MOBILE APP', one-line body, and pill button 'Open PWA ↗'; (3) anchor has correct href=https://jambh-ell.vercel.app, target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer; (4) clicking the button opens a new tab and does NOT navigate the current page; (5) testID='web-preview-info' and testID='webview-root' both present; (6) no console/page runtime errors; (7) /app/backend folder does not exist (as intended). All 7 requested checks passed.
critical_code_review_comments: index.tsx uses React.createElement('a', ...) which only renders on web (Platform.OS==='web'). This is correct usage since native builds use the WebView branch. No fix required.
Anchor style uses fontWeight: 700 (numeric) in a plain JS object passed to a raw <a> element — this is fine on the DOM side (converts to CSS 700).
No deprecated Expo packages detected (expo-network, expo-router, expo-splash-screen, react-native-webview all current).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure Expo WebView wrapper for https://jambh-ell.vercel.app. On web (Platform.OS==='web') it shows a small centered card (WebPreviewCard) with an HTML <a data-testid='open-pwa-in-browser'> that opens the PWA in a new tab. On native it renders the WebView. No backend exists — do not test /api. Use document.querySelector('a[data-testid="open-pwa-in-browser"]') for the button. Preview URL from frontend/.env: EXPO_PACKAGER_PROXY_URL.
